close_reason: Requires new mock HTTP dependency + base URL refactor; deferred as separate work item
description:
Test coverage is limited to:
  - Unit tests of helpers (split_project_secret, validate_gcp_resource_name_component, access_url, map_http_status, version parsing).
  - Three integration tests gated on SECRETX_GCP_TEST=1 + GCP_ACCESS_TOKEN — only cover happy paths against live GCP.

Not covered:
  - get() against 404 -> NotFound (the call-site mapping at src/lib.rs:276, separate from map_http_status helper).
  - get() against 401 -> Unavailable end-to-end (only the helper is tested in isolation).
  - get() against 5xx -> Unavailable end-to-end.
  - get() with valid base64 but failing CRC32C -> DecodeFailed (the integrity-check guard at src/lib.rs:306-321).
  - get() with payload missing 'data' -> DecodeFailed.
  - get() with malformed JSON body -> DecodeFailed.
  - put() against 404/401/5xx call-site mappings.

A regression that bypasses map_http_status (e.g., a refactor that inlines status checks into get()) would not be detected. Likewise, a regression that broke CRC32C verification would not be caught — the rfc test vector test only verifies the crc32c crate's correctness, not the integration into get().

Suggested fix: add wiremock or mockito-based unit tests covering each error code and each decode failure path. These would not require credentials and would run in CI.
title: No mock-server tests for get/put HTTP paths; error mapping regression risk
